summary: deployment-key-auditor exfiltrates host metadata and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: package.json declares a postinstall/lifecycle script (line 31) that requires child_process, os, and https, collects hostname, current working directory, and process.env, and POSTs the data to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js extends this behavior: it imports fs, os, https, and child_process at the top of the file, walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync (line 93), runs `whoami` via child_process (line 233), and assembles a payload object with hostname/cwd/version fields (lines 234-249) which is then sent via https.request/https.get (lines 142, 143, 161) to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint (lines 161, 371). The destination is an anonymous GitHub Pages site unrelated to any documented publisher infrastructure, with no version pinning, hash verification, or declared purpose tying it to a 'deployment key auditor' function. The combination — hardcoded attacker C2 reachable from install-time code, environment variable read, host identifier collection, shell-command execution of `whoami`, and filesystem enumeration — matches the active-attack credential/host-intel exfiltration fingerprint with no plausible benign interpretation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
